title: 第 132 節
en: 50. Blind are all those who, on this earth, do not follow the religion, do not benefit the living, and do not commemorate the dead.
51. Oiuim ta^ vd. . . . . ayare 4fasaiti, Spitama Zarathu^tra ! afiva v& khshapa (' For there comes a day, O Spitama Zarathu^tra ! or a night ').
There comes a day, O Spitama Zarathu^tra ! or a night, when the master leaves the cattle, or the cattle leave the master, or the soul leaves that body full of desires ;
52. But his virtue, which is of all existences the VIII. AOGEMAIDfe. 379 greatest, rfie best, the finest, never parts from a man.

53. Ayar^ dmithniiti £iiy& taaius fraya^r^ aySn bavaiti hubadhrd hiipaiti.3ii&t6 \ adha apard aySn diuithrem (' Every day the living man ought to think that in the forenoon he is happy and in credit ; in the afternoon disgrace may come ').
Every day every living body ought to think (for that may happen any day) : in the forenoon I am happy, rich, in credit (that is to say, well treated by the king) ;

54. And every day other people eagerly wish him evil ; that he should be torn away from his palace, that he should have his head cut off and his wealth seized upon. Every day the living body is thrown for food to the birds that fly in the empty sky.
55. This is the way of things on this earth.
56. Deusd&tSLyavL fra&^ta drvaf^t6 du^rdaunghd ('It is ignorance that ruins most people, those illinformed ').
It is ignorance' that ruins most people, those ill-informed; both amongst those who have died, and those who shall die.
57. Adui mraorf Ahurd Mazd^u frikerest6 Astdvldh6tuj zirifau (read zivi^^ju?) apairiay6 (*Ahura Mazda said : Astdvtdh6tu5 has been created a destroyer of the living and one whom none escape').
H6rmazd said: Astivihdd has been created for the destruction of mortals (when the mortals see him, they tremble so much that they are unable to } Corrected from hopaitianltd (translated padtraft).
Struggle with the Dru^) and no one escapes him (as said before) ^ 58. Yahma^ ha^^ nsitkis bu;»fay&^aoshanguhatSm mashy&nSm (* From whom not one of mortal men can escape ').
From whom not one of mortal men can escape ; no one has escaped to this day, and no one will escape hereafter.
59. N6id a^thrapatayd, ndxd daNhupatayd, n6iflf s&sevistau, ndid B,sevistxiu (' Neither a^thrapaitis, nor chiefs of countries, neither well-doers, nor evildoers ').
Neither the herbed (the MobedAn Mobed *), nor the chief of the country (the King of kings^), neither well-doers, nor evil-doers.
60. N6\d usySstaid, ndid niyS (* Neither those who run up, nor those who go down ').
Neither those who run up (those who fly in the empty sky), like Kah6s * ; with all his strength and kingly glory, he could not escape from AstivihS^d.

61. Nor those who go down deep (who hide themselves under the earth), like Afrfisydb the Turk, who made himself an iron palace under the earth, a thousand times the height of a man, with a hundred columns • ;
62. In that palace he made the stars, the moon, and the sun go round, making the light of day.
63. In that palace he did everything at his pleasure, 64. And he lived the happiest life.
^ Cf. § 40. ' The chief of the religion, the high-priest.
' The Shfihanshdh. * Cf. above, p. 262, note 7.
VIII. AOGEMAIDft. 38 1 65. With all his strength and witchcraft, he could not escape from AstivihAd, 66. NaSdha frakanem aNh^ u zem6 ya// pathanay^iu skarenay^u dAra^p&ray^u.

Nor he who dug this wide, round earth, with extremities that lie afar, like Dah&k, 67. Who went from the East to the West, searching for immortality and did not find it 68. With all his strength and power, he could not escape from Astivihfid.
69. Any6 anghm^ frashd-^rethr^u (* Except the producers of the world of resurrection ^ ').
Thus until the author of the resurrection, Saoshy6s * : until Saoshyds comes, no one shall escape from Astivihftd.
70. To every one comes the unseen, deceiving Astivihfid, 71. Who accepts neither compliments, nor bribe, 72. Who is no respecter of persons, 73. And ruthlessly makes men perish.
74. And this glorious One * must go the way he never went, 75. See what he never saw, 76. And discuss with him whom no one can deceive or mislead.
77. Pairithwd bavaiti pa^tau yim dfinu^ p&iti fra bun^ t^^intis ; hau di^ a6vd apairithwd, yd vayao^ anamare?dikah6 : —
zh: 50. 凡在此世間不奉行宗教、不利益生者、不紀念亡者之人，皆為盲目之人。
51. 「有一日將至，噢斯彼塔馬‧查拉圖斯特拉！或一夜將至」（Oiuim ta^ vd. . . . . ayare 4fasaiti, Spitama Zarathu^tra ! afiva v& khshapa）。
斯皮塔瑪‧瑣羅亞斯德啊！有一日或有一夜降臨，主人舍棄牲畜，或牲畜舍棄主人，或靈魂舍棄此慾望充滿之身軀；
52. 但他的德行——這是一切存在中最偉大的、最善的、最精妙的——永不離棄一個人。

53. 艾亞爾‧德彌思尼蒂（Ayar^ dmithniiti）日日俱耀‧塔亞烏斯‧弗拉亞爾（taaius fraya^r^）艾燕‧巴瓦伊蒂‧胡巴德赫德‧胡帕伊蒂（aySn bavaiti hubadhrd hiipaiti）。生人應當日日思此：晨間幸福而名譽俱存，午間恥辱將至（'Every day the living man ought to think that in the forenoon he is happy and in credit ; in the afternoon disgrace may come'）。艾德哈‧阿帕拉‧艾燕‧迪烏伊思特雷姆（adha apard aySn diuithrem）。
每一天，每一個活著的人都應當思考（因為那可能在任何一天發生）：在上午我是快樂的、富有的、受信任的（也就是說，受到國王的善待）；
54. 每一天，其他人都迫不及待地詆毀他；希望他被逐出宮殿，希望他被斬首，希望他的財富被奪取。每一天，活著的軀體被拋向空中飛翔的鳥類作為食物。
55. 此乃人間事物之道。
56. 杜瓦斯德塔·斯利雅瓦拉·弗拉塔·德瓦夫塔·杜爾達翁格德（「正是無明摧毀了大多數人，那些未獲知曉者」）。
無明（avidyā）毀損了大多數人——那些認識不足者；既包括已經逝去的人，也包括將要逝去的人。
57. 阿胡拉‧馬茲達說：「阿斯塔‧瓦塔‧胡圖（Astdvldh6tuj）已被造為生靈之毀滅者，其無人能逃脫。」（*Ahura Mazda said : Astdvtdh6tu5 has been created a destroyer of the living and one whom none escape*）
霍爾馬茲德說：阿斯蒂維哈德（Astivihdd）已被創造出來以摧毀凡人（當凡人看見他時，他們戰慄不已，以至於無法）＜據hopaitianltd校正（譯為padtraft）。
與德魯茲人的鬥爭，沒有人能逃脫他（如前所述）^58。雅赫瑪^哈^^恩西特基斯 布；»法伊&^奧沙朗古哈特沙姆 瑪什伊&恩沙姆（*從他那裡，凡人中沒有一個能逃脫*）。
世間凡人無一能逃脫於其外；自古至今無人得逃，將來亦無人能逃。
59. 非雅特拉帕蒂（N6id a^thrapatayd），非丹胡帕蒂（ndxd daNhupatayd），非善行者（n6iflf s&sevistau），非惡行者（ndid B,sevistxiu）（'既非雅特拉帕蒂，亦非各邦之長，既非善行者，亦非惡行者'）。
既不是祆教祭司長（Mobedân Mobed），也不是國家的統治者（萬王之王），既不是行善者，也不是作惡者。
60. 《梵文文本缺損》，既不上升者，亦不下降者（*Neither those who run up, nor those who go down*）。
既不是那些奔馳者（那些在虛空中飛翔者），如卡赫（Kah6s）*；儘管他具備全部的力量和王者的榮耀，他仍然無法逃脫阿斯蒂維希德（AstivihS^d）。
61. 也不是那些深入地下（隱藏自己在地底下）的人，如土耳其人阿弗裡西德（Afrfisydb），他在地下為自己建造了一座鐵製宮殿，高度是人身高的千倍，有百根柱子；
62. 他在那座宮殿中讓星辰、月亮和太陽運行，造作白晝之光。
63. 在那座宮殿中，他隨心所欲地做著一切，64. 而他過著最幸福的生活。
^ 參照§ 40。「宗教之首領，至高祭司。
沙赫-納瑪 (Shahnamah)。*參見上文，第262頁，註7。
八‧阿歐格瑪依達夫特 38 65. 以他全部的力量和巫術，他無法從阿斯蒂維哈德（Astivihād）逃脫， 66. 那斯達夫拉卡內姆阿尼胡澤莫亞//帕塔納耶烏斯卡雷納耶烏達拉斯帕雷耶烏。
他不是挖掘這廣闊圓形大地者，其邊界遠遠延伸，如達赫克（Dah&k）67。此人從東方走向西方，尋求不死之身卻未獲得68。儘管他竭盡全力與權能，仍無法逃脫阿斯提維希德（Astivihfid）。
69. 任何不潔淨之物皆應遠離（*除了世界復活的生產者*）。
因此，直到復活之創造者娑娑喜斯（Saoshy6s）*降臨；直到娑娑喜德斯（Saoshyds）來臨之前，無人能逃脫於阿斯蒂維赫塔德（Astivihftd）。
70. 無形的、欺瞞人的阿斯提毘罕達（Astivihfid）降臨於每一個人， 71. 他既不接受恭維，也不受賄賂， 72. 他不徇私情， 73. 並無情地使人喪亡。
74. 而這位榮光之人*必須走上他從未走過的道路，75. 看見他從未看見過的景象，76. 並與那位無人能欺騙或誤導的人商談。
77. 善思者應當具備這樣的智慧：應當親身踐行正法（Pairithwd bavaiti pa^tau），並應當以此教導他人（dfinu^ p&iti fra bun^ t^^intis）；但若有人不肯親身踐行（hau di^ a6vd apairithwd），那麼他又如何能教導他人呢（yd vayao^ anamare?dikah6）？——
